It was this man's misery that he needed to be carried in this way, and it shows the suffering state of human life; it was kind of those who carried him, and teaches the compassion that should be in people toward their fellow creatures in distress. True faith and strong faith may work in various ways; but it will be accepted and approved by Jesus Christ. Sin is the cause of all our pains and sicknesses. The way to remove the effect is to take away the cause. Forgiveness of sin strikes at the root of all diseases. Christ proved his power to forgive sin by showing his power to cure the man sick with palsy. And his curing diseases was a sign of his forgiving sin, for sin is the disease of the soul; when it is forgiven, it is healed. When we see what Christ does in healing souls, we must acknowledge that we have never seen anything like it. Most people think themselves whole; they feel no need of a physician, therefore despise or neglect Christ and his gospel. But the convinced, humbled sinner who despairs of all help except from the Savior will show his faith by applying to him without delay.

Matthew was not a good character, or else, being a Jew, he would never have been a publican, that is, a tax-gatherer for the Romans. However, Christ called this publican to follow him. With God, through Christ, there is mercy to forgive the greatest sins, and grace to change the greatest sinners, and make them holy. A faithful, fair-dealing publican was rare. And because the Jews had a particular hatred of an office which proved that they were subject to the Romans, they gave these tax-gatherers an ill name. But those like these our blessed Lord did not hesitate to converse with when he appeared in the likeness of sinful flesh. And it is no new thing for something that is both well done and well designed to be slandered, and turned to the reproach of the wisest and best of men. Christ would not withdraw even though the Pharisees were offended. If the world had been righteous, there would have been no occasion for his coming either to preach repentance or to purchase forgiveness. We must not keep company with ungodly people out of love for their empty conversation; but we are to show love to their souls, remembering that our good Physician had the power of healing in himself and was in no danger of taking the disease; but it is not so with us. In trying to do good to others, let us be careful we do not get harm to ourselves.

Strict professors are likely to blame all who do not fully agree with their own views. Christ did not escape slanders; we should be willing to bear them as well as careful not to deserve them; but should attend to every part of our duty in its proper order and season.

The Sabbath is a sacred and Divine institution; a privilege and benefit, not a task and drudgery. God never designed it to be a burden for us, therefore we must not make it so to ourselves. The Sabbath was instituted for the good of mankind, living in society, having many wants and troubles, preparing for a state of happiness or misery. Man was not made for the Sabbath, as if his keeping it could be of service to God, nor was he commanded to keep outward observances to his real hurt. Every observance respecting it is to be interpreted by the rule of mercy.
